cards.title: Two steps forward, one slip back
cards.caption: A quiet note on progress being messy — but real. Surfaced from the noise by Threadverse.
cards.quote: Social progress is mostly two steps forward and one slip back.

cards.title: Japan just OK'd joint custody after divorce
cards.caption: Kids get both parents. Japan approves joint custody after divorce for the first time — genuine good news. What law would you want passed next? (Surfaced by Threadverse.)
cards.quote: When finally something good happens in Japan politics, it's worth celebrating.

cards.title: Is happiness the way forward, not GDP?
cards.caption: Bhutan measures happiness, not GDP. Is that the future? An idea worth sitting with — pulled from the feed by Threadverse.
cards.quote: Is Gross National Happiness the way forward?

cards.title: Change, without alienating everyone
cards.caption: A case for progress with practical limits. One thread's take on moderation, surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: The remedy is structural, not just moral
cards.caption: Include women and gender-diverse people fully. Allow failure. Societies get richer and more resilient. A sharp take Threadverse pulled from the noise. Agree?
cards.quote: The remedy is structural, not just moral.

cards.title: New business models for real growth
cards.caption: On rethinking growth for sustainability. A HackerNews link Threadverse surfaced.

cards.title: Africa: from hope to collective action
cards.caption: The 2025 Global Social Progress Week, reimagining what's possible for a continent. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: £1m of teargas = £1m of housing to GDP
cards.caption: GDP counts teargas the same as affordable housing. Degrowth asks: what are we actually measuring? A line that reframes everything — Threadverse found it. Does GDP still mean anything?
cards.quote: £1m worth of teargas is exactly the same as £1m worth of affordable housing.
cards.povText: POV: you realize GDP counts teargas and housing the exact same way

cards.title: Progress isn't a straight line
cards.caption: We assume progress moves in a steady line. Research says we mostly just perceive it that way, even when the data disagrees. What progress felt slower than it was?
cards.quote: Individuals frequently perceive progress as steady and continuous, despite evidence suggesting otherwise.

cards.title: A scientist is running for Congress
cards.caption: "I know what happens when science gets attacked." A research scientist is running to bring a science voice to Congress. Surfaced this one from the noise. Root for her?
cards.quote: That's why I'm running to bring a science voice to Congress.

cards.title: Equality vs equity, revisited
cards.caption: The old equity cartoon, repurposed into an anti-equality argument. More manifesto than good news.

cards.title: Canada: 15th in the world for social progress
cards.caption: Canada ranks 15th globally on the Social Progress Index (USA sits 29th). 13 years of data behind it. Where did your country land?
cards.quote: Canada is considered a Tier 1 country according to the Global Social Progress Index.

cards.title: Ghana's poverty rate drops 3 quarters straight
cards.caption: Ghana's multidimensional poverty rate just fell for the third consecutive quarter. Real, measurable progress. The kind of headline that never trends but should.
cards.quote: Ghana's multidimensional poverty rate declines for third consecutive quarter.

cards.title: Small communities, real progress
cards.caption: A gentle case for community development as the engine of long-term social progress. Warm, but reads more like a blog intro than a moment.
cards.quote: Small community-focused efforts can contribute to stronger and more...

cards.title: The choreographed progress theory
cards.caption: A sharp but conspiratorial take on how rights movements are framed. Too charged and cryptic for a good-news feed.
cards.quote: Never forget that, especially not in the coming weeks.

cards.title: A reply lost in the mentions
cards.caption: A reply-chain fragment full of @mentions and a broken link. No context, no payoff.

cards.title: Just a livestream link
cards.caption: A retweet that's mostly links and a hashtag — no context, no payoff. Skipping this one.

cards.title: Conservatism feeds on loneliness
cards.caption: "In a labor market where the future isn't guaranteed, it's easier for the extreme right to triumph." A line worth sitting with. Which platform surfaced this for you? Threadverse did for us.
cards.quote: Conservatism feeds on fear, uncertainty and loneliness

cards.title: Paid leave, free childcare, real wins
cards.caption: Quietly, the wins are stacking up: NY paid prenatal leave, NM free childcare, the bipartisan Take It Down Act. Progress that doesn't trend. Which one surprised you? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.povText: POV: the good legislation nobody put in your feed
